entries.title: Lady Nagant Is Sent By AFO

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One sends Lady Nagant to capture Deku and exploit her disillusionment with hero society.
content.injection: Nagant should enter as an AFO-directed hunter with her own bitterness, not as a random mercenary.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

content.fact: Lady Nagant once killed for the Hero Public Safety Commission, giving her insider knowledge of hero society’s hidden violence.
content.injection: When Nagant explains her past, let it expose institutional rot without making every hero personally complicit.

content.fact: Nagant’s cynicism comes from real corruption and dirty work, not from simple villainous spite.
content.injection: Deku should have to answer a credible moral wound in hero society, not just defeat an evil sniper.

entries.title: Nagant Rifle Threatens From Extreme Range

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Lady Nagant’s Rifle makes distance and line of sight lethal before Deku can close in.
content.injection: Use Nagant’s range to pressure movement, cover, and timing; close combat must not be automatic.

entries.title: Nagant Uses Air Walk From AFO

content.fact: All For One grants Lady Nagant Air Walk, improving her angles and mobility during the fight.
content.injection: Nagant can reposition in the air, but Air Walk is an added tool from AFO, not her original Quirk.

entries.title: Overhaul Is Broken And Fixated On Boss

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Chisaki is psychologically diminished and fixated on apologizing to his boss after losing power and hands.
content.injection: Post-prison Chisaki should feel ruined and obsessive, not restored to Hassaikai leadership.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

content.fact: With his arms gone, Chisaki cannot function as the same Overhaul threat he was during the Hassaikai raid.
content.injection: Do not give Chisaki casual Overhaul combat ability in this arc unless the story has explicitly changed his condition.

content.fact: Deku recognizes that Nagant is not simply evil and tries to reach the person behind her cynicism.
content.injection: Deku’s response should combine tactical urgency with mercy; he is trying to understand and save, not only win.

content.fact: Deku uses Fa Jin and other OFA tools to simulate a 100 percent-level burst without simply using old full-power Smash damage.
content.injection: Treat Faux 100 Percent as a layered technique with setup and timing, not permanent safe full-power OFA.

entries.title: Nagant Tests Deku Ideals Under Fire

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Nagant challenges Deku’s belief that hero society can still be saved while actively trying to capture him.
content.injection: Let the fight be ideological and tactical at once; dialogue should pressure Deku’s ideals while bullets pressure his body.

entries.title: AFO Turns Nagant Into A Bomb

context.label: All For One turns Nagant into an explosive trap

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One planted a failsafe in Nagant, detonating her when she turns toward Deku’s side.
content.injection: Use the explosion as AFO’s cruelty and contingency planning; Nagant’s change of heart must not make her safe.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

content.fact: Before collapsing, Nagant gives Deku information that leads him toward All For One’s mansion trap.
content.injection: Nagant’s clue should move the investigation forward while still leaving AFO in control of the next trap.

entries.title: Mansion Message Shows AFO Psychological Warfare

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The mansion trap is built to taunt Deku and deepen his isolation rather than to start the final battle.
content.injection: AFO’s message should escalate psychological pressure; do not turn the mansion into the Final War confrontation.

entries.title: Nagant Is Critically Injured Not Fully Restored

context.label: All For One turns Nagant into an explosive trap

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After AFO’s trap, Nagant survives only in a critical, medically fragile state.
content.injection: Do not put Nagant back into normal field support immediately after the explosion; later aid must stay Context-gated.

entries.title: Deku Mercy Is Risky But Consistent

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Deku’s mercy toward Nagant reflects his core heroic instinct even when it creates risk.
content.injection: Deku should remain willing to reach for enemies with visible pain; that mercy is a strength under strain, not naivety alone.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Overhaul Has No Hassaikai Command Here
